blockingIssues.description: `fresh_db_save_requires_no_manual_define_table` reuses `ensure_db()` instead of reinitializing a fresh embedded database, so it does not prove first-save behavior on a clean DB as required by VAL-SAVE-001.
`Repo::save` still commits before `T::hydrate_foreign(stored).await` and repairs failures with post-commit deletes, violating the required true commit-boundary atomicity for VAL-SAVE-004.
The single-save regression only proves residue cleanup after a forced hydrate failure; it does not prove that the public error path cannot expose already-committed state without relying on repair.

`Repo::save_many` still commits before hydrating returned rows and then compensates by deleting parents and tracked children on failure, so batch atomicity and retry semantics still depend on post-commit cleanup instead of an authoritative transaction boundary.
The batch/retry feature depends on the same underlying `Repo::save` path, which still uses post-commit cleanup, so VAL-SAVE-005 and VAL-CROSS-002 remain unmet at the commit-boundary level.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that fresh-DB regressions must actively reset or reinitialize the embedded database rather than reusing shared helpers like `ensure_db()` that may carry prior schema state.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of `milestone-a-fresh-save-and-explicit-id-foreign-persist` found the committed fresh-db test at `core/tests/integration_db.rs:994-1013` uses `ensure_db()` and therefore does not establish a clean first-save precondition.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen atomicity worker guidance to require code-level proof that commit occurs only after failure-prone hydrate/return-shaping work is no longer possible, rather than accepting residue-cleanup tests as sufficient evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both commit-boundary reviews found `Repo::save`/`Repo::save_many` still commit before hydration and rely on compensating deletes, yet prior worker handoffs treated passing cleanup regressions as success.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify shared GitNexus fallback guidance so workers may use pure `git diff`-based review when no GitNexus tooling is available at all, and distinguish that case from partial-tool fallback.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple worker handoffs reported no available `gitnexus_*` commands in this environment and fell back to `git diff`/`git status` inspection.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS guidance should be updated to restate that post-commit compensating deletes are forbidden for atomicity.
